nodes.label: Noise and flatness — where does robustness live? (existential)
nodes.rationale: Phase 4's A7 flagged eval-time weight-noise sensitivity as the sharpest open silicon risk; asks whether robustness has to be absorbed in SCFF itself or can the GD readout absorb it — and if not, the whole arc must be rethought.

nodes.label: Bishop 1995: input noise = Tikhonov (Jacobian smoothness)
nodes.rationale: Injecting small-variance input noise during training adds, to first order, a Jacobian-smoothness (Tikhonov) penalty; the channel for noise on the features the readout reads (SCFF taps, ADC quantization) — the right citation only for input/activation noise, not weight noise.

nodes.label: LP-FT: a frozen backbone caps head robustness
nodes.rationale: A trained head preserves but cannot manufacture robustness the features lack; if A7's sensitivity originates in SCFF's representation, training the readout with noise cannot fix it — the fix must be upstream in the SCFF objective. This is why noise is a Stage-1, not Stage-2, problem.

nodes.label: The literature prior favors the NO branch (sensitivity is upstream)
nodes.rationale: LP-FT + Rasch jointly predict the NO branch: if sensitivity originates in SCFF's representation AND the probed channel (weight) isn't even the dominant one (Rasch says tap/ADC dominates), the readout can't fix it and Phase 9 could declare victory against the weaker enemy — pre-budget for the harder branch, don't treat it as unlikely tail.
